column1: flow
user -> ranking — type=«is superato» ; label="ha superato" ; explanation="a user can be superated by a ranking"
mission -> tip — type=«è completata» ; label="ha completata" ; explanation="a mission can be completed by a tip"
user -> ranking — type=«is superato» ; label="ha superato"
this diagram models a system where users can be ranked, missions can be completed by tips.
the "ha superato" relationship indicates a user is ranked.
the "ha completata" relationship indicates a mission is completed by a tip.
main -> firebase authentication — type=synchronous message; label="signinwithemailandpassword(string, string)"; explanation="main calls firebase authentication's signinwithemailandpassword method"
firebase authentication -> main — type=return message; label="response"; explanation="firebase authentication returns a response"
main -> main — type=synchronous message; label="if (response != error) { return firebaseauthuser }"; explanation="main checks the response and returns firebaseauthuser if no error"
main calls firebase authentication with signinwithemailandpassword(string, string).
firebase authentication returns a response.
main checks if response != error.
if response != error, main returns firebaseauthuser.
* the diagram models the login process between a main application and firebase authentication.
* the main flow involves the main application initiating the login process with firebase authentication.
* the success path returns a user object, while an error results in no return value (implicitly).
main -> createuserwithemailandpassword — type=message; label="createuserwithemailandpassword"; explanation="calls the createuserwithemailandpassword activity";
createuserwithemailandpassword -> db.child(userid) — type=message; label="setvalue(hashmap<string, object>)"; explanation="sets the userid in the database";
createuserwithemailandpassword -> login — type=message; label="login"; explanation="calls the login activity";
createuserwithemailandpassword -> main — type=message; label="return firebaseuser, userid"; explanation="returns firebaseuser and userid";
db.child(userid) -> createuserwithemailandpassword — type=message; label="response"; explanation="checks the response from the login activity";
db.child(userid) -> crea response — type=message; label="crea response"; explanation="sends the response to the crea response activity";
login -> crea response — type=message; label="crea response"; explanation="sends the response to the crea response activity";
flow 1: email.isempty() -> createuserwithemailandpassword
flow 2: password.length() < 8 -> createuserwithemailandpassword
flow 3: createuserwithemailandpassword -> db.child(userid).setvalue(hashmap<string, object>)
flow 4: db.child(userid).setvalue(hashmap<string, object>) -> login
flow 5: login -> crea response
the diagram models the registration process, specifically the steps involved in creating a new user in firebase authentication.
the process checks for empty email and minimum password length.
it involves creating a user in the database and then attempting to log in.
the diagram shows a potential error path where the login fails.
main -> realtime database — message="hashmap<string, object>"; explanation="sends hashmap";
realtime database -> main — message="conversione da hashmap a json"; explanation="converts to json";
realtime database -> main — message="crea response"; explanation="creates response";
main -> main — message="gestisce la risposta"; explanation="handles response";
main sends a hashmap to the realtime database.
the realtime database converts the hashmap to a json.
the realtime database sends a response to the main.
main handles the response.
the main component initiates the process by sending a hashmap to the realtime database.
the realtime database transforms the data into json format.
the process concludes with the main component handling the received response.
the diagram highlights an asynchronous communication pattern between the main and realtime database components.
main -> firebase sdk: addvalueeventlistener("mission");
firebase sdk -> realtime database: addvalueeventlistener("mission");
realtime database -> firebase sdk: controllo il token e raccoglie i dati richiesti
firebase sdk -> realtime database: manda la risposta con i dati richiesti
realtime database -> firebase sdk: controllo il token e raccoglie i dati richiesti
the diagram models a sequence of operations between a main application, a firebase sdk, and a realtime database.
the main application initiates a listener for "mission" events.
the firebase sdk then adds a listener to the realtime database to receive data related to those missions.
the realtime database checks a token and collects the requested data.
finally, the firebase sdk sends the collected data back to the main application.
